gate.test_metric_label: as-built E[T]=2.1 on the depth-9 step (AWAITING LIVE ORACLE RUN; fp32 fix targets E[T]->5.207 -> 537.8)
gate.verdict_label: the tree LIVES, conditional on fixing BOTH bugs. As-built E[T]=2.621 -> official 271 (RED, fails 500 by 229). The both-bugs-fixed cell (depth-1->0.7287 + rho-optimal descent) realizes E[T]=5.207 -> official 537.8 (>= 500 by +37.8, conservative corner 536.9). BUILD TARGET: the banked M=32/depth-9/max-branch-3 rho-optimal tree (parent array banked, #125). DECISIVE LEVER = BUG-2/descent: fixing it alone reaches 522; fixing only BUG-1 reaches just 283. CONDITIONAL on the deeper-spine ladder recovering (162 TPS of the cell); if the fix restores depth-1 but not the deeper spine it lands 376 (RED).
gate.rule: PR #134 LIVE: GREEN = both-bugs-fixed cell clears 500 (tree has a named, quantified path) / AMBER = straddles 499-501 (tau/step knife-edge) / RED = both-bugs-fixed < 500 even at the rho-optimal ceiling (tree dead).

finding_depth9_bar.title: depth-9 operative clear-500 bar is HIGHER than the demand floor
finding_depth9_bar.explanation: The board-merged demand floor 4.624 (denken #123) is the TOPOLOGY-OPTIMAL E[T] to clear 500 -- it corresponds to a ~depth-6.7 tree (step 1.1585, between the banked depth-6 bar 4.553 and depth-7 bar 4.649). The clear-500 bar RISES with realized depth (each step costs more). The build land #71 ships is the depth-9 W* tree (to REACH the 5.207 ceiling), whose step is 1.2128, so it needs accept_length >= 4.841 to clear 500. At E[T]=4.624 the depth-9 tree only yields 477.6 < 500. => the depth-9 build bracket is [4.841, 5.207], NOT [4.624, 5.207]; the low-end slack is 0.366 E[T], not 0.583. The live-oracle gate should use 4.841 (or re-price step at the oracle's reported realized depth via step_time_for_depth).

synthetic_oracle.source: SELF-TEST (banked rho-optimal ladder as a perfect oracle)

consistency.note: reconstructed E[T] from the oracle's q-ladder on the banked M=32 topology should match the oracle's accept_length within tol; a gap means accept_length was mis-measured or the topology differs.

greedy_exact.mechanism: fp32 star-verify (QK+PV -> fp32/IEEE, relerr 1e-3 -> 1e-6) makes ta[0] == true target argmax -> tree greedy-EXACT by construction; does NOT inherit kanna #114's 56% bf16-near-tie-flip divergence.

input.ran_on: openevolve unlimited A10G (no PPL, no bench-quota)
input._schema_note: LIVE oracle readout. per_position_cumulative_accept[d] = MARGINAL P(committed path reaches >= depth d). E[T] = 1 (bonus) + sum(cumulative). This is the CUMULATIVE form (chain-identity reconstruct), distinct from the synthetic self-test sample which stores per-position CONDITIONAL q_spine. depth1_spine_accept is the depth-1 top-1 spine match (tree-attn-free / linear-identical forward).

bugs_live.BUG_1_spine_depth1: 0.679 < 0.7287 fp32 target (denken #133 lane: positions / fused spec metadata / drafter conditioning at depth-1)
bugs_live.BUG_2_salvage_no_descend: E[T]=2.621 below even the 3.844 linear-MTP floor; salvage fires (391) + eager-correct (full=37) but does not descend (wirbel lane)

input.oracle_read: the bug is at depth-1 (spine top-1), NOT the star-attn verify (which works). Make depth-1 spine byte-identical to linear first; salvage is then additive on top.
input.discrepancy_note: chiku-inu's separate probe (board 20260614-104247-994) reports depth1_accept=0.591 / tok/step=2.07 for the same tree-488-pw-fp32-v0; openevolve's A10G oracle (this file, the PR-authoritative source) reports 0.679 / 2.621. Both agree qualitatively: fp32 did NOT fix BUG-1; as-built FAILS 500 (official 214-271). The both-bugs-fixed conclusion is invariant to which readout is canonical.

step1_measured_verdict.note: E[T]=1+sum(cumulative)=2.5440 reconstructs the oracle accept_length=2.621 within residual +0.0770 (3.03%); the DP linear cross-check agrees (2.5440). The small positive residual is the weak as-built salvage the bare-spine cumulative omits -- itself BUG-2 evidence (salvage adds only ~0.077 tok/step, not the ~2.6 the rho-optimal descent must).

cell2_spineFixed_salvageAsMeasured.label: only BUG-1 fixed (depth-1->0.7287, salvage still broken)

cell3_spineMeas_salvageRhoOpt.label: only BUG-2 fixed (rho-opt descent, depth-1 still 0.679)

both_bugs_fixed_load_bearing_sensitivity.note: The both-bugs-fixed 538 ASSUMES the deeper spine (depths 2-9) recovers to the deployed/rho-optimal ladder, per openevolve's read ('make depth-1 byte-identical to linear; salvage additive on top'). If the depth-1+descent fix does NOT also restore the deeper spine (measured conditional ~[0.52,0.58,..] << rho-opt [0.76,0.79,..]), the cell lands at 376 (RED). This deeper-ladder recovery (worth 162 TPS) is the single most decision-relevant input -- wirbel's re-bench after the fix must confirm the FULL cumulative ladder recovers, not just depth-1.

step3_greedy_exact.semantics: greedy-EXACT w.r.t. its OWN M=32 verify (fp32 ta[0]==M=32 argmax by construction)
step3_greedy_exact.CAVEAT_not_spec_eq_AR: NOT spec==M=1-AR identity. kanna #122 (MERGED) localized the M-variance to the int4 Marlin GEMM (no batch-invariant Marlin in the wheel), so M=32-verify argmax != M=1-AR argmax. The tree's greedy-exactness is w.r.t. its OWN M=32 verify; PPL validity rides on the Issue #124 ruling, NOT on spec==AR. Do not claim spec==AR identity.
step3_greedy_exact.interpretation: openevolve localized the defect to depth-1 spine (the tree-attn-free / linear-identical forward), NOT the star-attn verify ('which works'). So accepted tokens are the true M=32 argmax (verify exact); the profile is degraded only in match RATE. The Step-2 ideal-profile match returns 0 (depth-1 0.679<0.7287) -- that flags the DRAFTER/build degradation, it does NOT impugn the fp32 verify's exactness.

step4_existential.build_target: M=32 / depth-9 / max-branch-3 rho-optimal tree (parent array banked, #125)

The both-bugs-fixed cell (depth-1->0.7287 + rho-optimal descent) realizes E[T]=5.207 -> official 537.8 (>= 500 by +37.8, conservative corner 536.9). BUILD TARGET: the banked M=32/depth-9/max-branch-3 rho-optimal tree (parent array banked, #125). DECISIVE LEVER = BUG-2/descent: fixing it alone reaches 522; fixing only BUG-1 reaches just 283. CONDITIONAL on the deeper-spine ladder recovering (162 TPS of the cell); if the fix restores depth-1 but not the deeper spine it lands 376 (RED).

oracle_input_schema.depth1_spine_accept: float -- depth-1 spine accept q_1 (fp32 target 0.7287) [#1]

provenance: ingests the openevolve A10G-oracle 4-number readout of tree-488-pw-fp32-v0 and maps it through the #100 lever_composition compose (K_cal) + my #125 tree_et_realization_ceiling step model (W* depth-9 step, measured 1.83x attn tax) + wirbel treeshape_measured_accept E[T] DP (consistency). Demand floor 4.624 + tree-free 491.8 from denken #123 (board-merged); fp32-greedy-exact mechanism from chiku-inu (board 20260614-092043-711) + kanna #114.
method: LOCAL CPU-only analytic readout harness; no GPU/vLLM/HF Job/submission/kernel build. Banks the live-oracle pipe. Greedy identity untouched.
